original_abstract:
The cultivation of religious character is a fundamental pillar of individual development, serving as the basis for an individual's ethical conduct, speech, and adherence to spiritual principles. Within the Indonesian Islamic boarding school (*pesantren*) system, the practice of *mujahadah*—a form of earnest spiritual struggle and persistence—acts as a vital pedagogical tool for character building. This study investigates the implementation and impact of *mujahadah* activities on the religious character of students (*santri*).

By adopting a qualitative library research methodology, this study analyzes relevant literature, including academic journals and books, to explore how these spiritual rituals influence student personality. The findings indicate that *mujahadah* is far more than a repetitive ritual; it is a holistic learning process that integrates spiritual, social, and psychological dimensions. Through consistent practice, students develop key virtues such as steadfastness in worship, heightened divine consciousness, social sensitivity, and a strong sense of personal responsibility. Furthermore, the role of the *Kyai* (religious leader) as a role model and the structured environment of the *pesantren* act as crucial facilitators in this developmental process. 

The study concludes that *mujahadah* is a highly effective method for character education that fosters independence and integrity. These insights suggest that educational institutions rooted in Islamic values can optimize such spiritual activities to strengthen character formation strategies, ensuring that students not only master religious theory but also internalize moral values in their daily lives.
humanized_abstract:
TO OUTFIT AN INDIVIDUAL WITH A RELIGIOUS CHARACTER IS TO OUTFIT HIM AS A PERSON, TO FORM HIM AS A HUMAN BEING WHO IS ABLE TO STRIVE towards Bлагость in all aspects of his life: in his deeds, in his words and in the entirety of his religious experience. In the context of the pesantren system of Indonesian Islamic schools the practices of mujahadah is a method or pedagogy of character formation. The aim of this study is to observe and understand the implementation of mujahadah practices and their implications for the religious character of santri students.

The researcher employed qualitative library research methodology that involves analyzing data in the form of academic journals and literature. The data was analyzed to investigate the effects of the spiritual rituals on the students personality. The result of the research showed that that the mujahadah practice was not a monotonous ritual but was a process of learning which enhanced the students’ spiritual, social and psychological aspects. By performing the mujahadah practices in Madrasah Darul Mawa’ bid, students were able to cultivate and increase their spiritual worship and divine conscience as well as enhance their socialization and sense of responsibility. In addition, the roles played by Kyai in serving as a role model to the students as well as the role of pesantren environment facilitated the desired outcome of the students’ mujahadah practices.

The study concludes that *mujahadah* is very effective for carrying out character education activities that foster independence and integrity. Therefore, it is suggested that an Islamic school, as a system of education that is based on Islamic values, is expected to be able to maximize spiritual activities to enhance character education. So that students are not only able to understand the theoretical concepts of Islamic education, but can also apply Islamic values in their daily lives.
